note: Beautiful historic quarter paved in uneven cobblestones with some slopes down to the waterfront; charming but jarring for wheels and strollers, though shops and the waterfront promenade are flatter.
A ramped accessible entrance and largely flat nave, but the cobbled square outside and some interior steps complicate access; the light show is seated. Partly accessible.

The mountain park has some accessible paved paths and a shuttle/road to the Kondiaronk lookout, but many trails are steep gravel or stairs. Take the road/shuttle up; then the lookout terrace is accessible.
Fully accessible across its pavilions with lifts, step-free entrances, loaner wheelchairs and free collection entry. Flat and easy.
A large, mostly flat open-air/covered market at ground level with wide aisles; step-free and navigable though busy on weekends. Good accessible food stop.
A vast climate-controlled network linking downtown with elevators and level passages throughout; a genuinely accessible way to move around, especially in winter. Some elevators tucked away.
The riverside promenade is flat and largely paved/step-free with ramps, distinct from the cobbled streets behind it; easy and scenic.
St. Joseph's Oratory has lifts and an accessible route to the main levels but sits on a hill with many external steps; use the accessible entrance and internal lifts. Partly accessible.
The Biodôme and Olympic Park facilities are modern and largely accessible with lifts and step-free routes; the tower funicular is accessible. Good for families.
A vibrant neighborhood to wander for murals and cafes; mostly flat sidewalks but famous for outdoor spiral staircases and the odd hill, and some shops have steps. Sidewalk strolling is fine.
A flat covered market with lift access and wide aisles beside the accessible Lachine Canal path; easy and pleasant.
